title: libquiv-aging error code registry
description: Fact-layer schema for the offline-workflow error-code registry. Every code referenced by scripts, runbooks or PARAMETERS.json must validate against this schema.

codes.description: Map of error code ID to code definition. Keys must match pattern and match the entry's scope prefix.

trigger.description: Condition under which the code fires. Order-of-magnitude thresholds only; absolute numerical tolerances are forbidden.

consequence.description: Physical or methodological impact of ignoring this condition.

cross_refs.description: Pointers to authoritative documents. Use 'TODO: ...' when a precise section/page is not yet known; entries must not be empty.

remediation.description: Ordered list of field-level remediations, highest-priority first. Must be non-empty for status=active entries (enforced by test, not schema).

escalation.description: Pointer into docs/08_consultation_protocol.md (section id or anchor).

status.description: draft = placeholder spec pending script-side validation; not yet consumed by scripts.

deprecated_note.description: Present only when status=deprecated (conditional enforcement is done in test).

notes.description: Free-form commentary (e.g. why an entry is draft, caveats for interpretation).
